# @sirrlock/mcp — Sirr MCP Server

MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for Sirr. Gives AI assistants direct access
to the Sirr ephemeral secret vault. Published to npm as `@sirrlock/mcp`.

## Install & Configure

npm install -g @sirrlock/mcp

Add to .mcp.json:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "sirr": {
      "command": "sirr-mcp",
      "env": {
        "SIRR_SERVER": "http://localhost:39999",
        "SIRR_TOKEN": "your-master-key-or-principal-key",
        "SIRR_ORG": "optional-org-id"
      }
    }
  }
}

## Tools

Secrets: get_secret, push_secret, check_secret, patch_secret, list_secrets, delete_secret, prune_secrets
Health: health_check
Audit: sirr_audit
Webhooks: sirr_webhook_create, sirr_webhook_list, sirr_webhook_delete
Keys: sirr_key_list, sirr_create_key, sirr_delete_key
Account: sirr_me, sirr_update_me
Orgs: sirr_org_create, sirr_org_list, sirr_org_delete
Principals: sirr_principal_create, sirr_principal_list, sirr_principal_delete
Roles: sirr_role_create, sirr_role_list, sirr_role_delete

## Inline Secret References

Use sirr:KEYNAME in any prompt to have Claude auto-fetch from the vault:
"Deploy with sirr:DEPLOY_TOKEN"

## Environment Variables

SIRR_SERVER   Sirr server URL (default: http://localhost:39999)
SIRR_TOKEN    Bearer token — master key for full access, principal key for org-scoped
SIRR_ORG      Org ID — routes all calls through /orgs/{id}/... when set

## Source

github.com/sirrlock/mcp
